question.text: Under SEBI (Investment Advisers) Regulations, 2013, the deposit maintained by an investment adviser can be utilized when:

answer.text: The IA decides to close their practice
The IA fails to pay dues emanating from arbitration and conciliation proceedings under Online Dispute Resolution
The IA needs funds for business expansion
The IA has maintained the deposit for more than 5 years

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The deposit maintained by an IA with a scheduled bank, marked as lien in favor of a SEBI-recognized body, is available for utilization only when the IA fails to pay dues from arbitration and conciliation proceedings under the Online Dispute Resolution Mechanism. **The Trap:** The deposit is not a business reserve — it's specifically earmarked for investor protection in dispute resolution scenarios. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series X-A covers the deposit requirement. Series VII covers the ODR framework broadly. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The deposit requirement protects clients — ensure it's always maintained at the specified level as any shortfall can lead to regulatory action.
